Retail sales are stagnant | Intraday post (10/Feb)

Weekly post:

Python course is launched | Weekly post (09-13/Feb)

I’m happy to announce that the Python course is now available on my website for all paid subscribers. See details by clicking on the link.

Retail sales is stagnant that is a slight negative surprise. But remember to the weekly post: “bad news is good news”, supporting for soft-landing and market wants to force the cut.

For yesterday, the weekly post predicted: “Monday: cautious risk-appetite, false breakouts, benign stabilization exists but tends to show up as reversal mechanics, not smooth trend.” It played out well. For today, it predicts choppiness. (See levels below). Watch ES at 6960/70 band as line-in-the-sand, and 7005/7010 as upside pivot line.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The net speed profile is negative, customers are buying volatility into both direction, but upside vol is 2.34x more bid, while upside vol of vol being sold. There is no downside vol selling! So the pivot matters pretty much.

Centroid is in the 6967.15 - 6978.25 zone. This is a sticky zone.

Downside pivot is at 6927.90with target of 6894.22At the target, vol can catch up locally taking spot to 6860/70 zone only to revert back up to the pivot and pin there.

Upside pivot is at 6995.49with target of 7009.98The pivot is hard to break. More probability to break it during AM session, then upside target is heavy pin. Otherwise the pivot is a rejection point.

The primal bet of the market is choppiness around the centroid, holding above the downside pivot and below the upside pivot. However, this trend would showup into the after-noon. Until London close, the pivots matter pretty much!

OptionsDepth Heatmap
